channel.title: Korean on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Korean on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Turntable Chicken Jazz
item.description: Summary The vibe here is pretty cool if you’re into old school jazz environments. The walls have a load of records and lots of old music devices. It does get a little noisy, and sometimes people eat really slow to create a wait. It’s not too different than other typical Korean bar/chicken joints where they have lots of alcohol and Korean bar foods. The drinks were ok. Started off with a passionfruit lemonade.

item.title: Anytime
item.description: Summary This place is interesting. It’s like a modern version of typical Korean tapas. They have these idea cocktails that have flashing lights underneath it. You can click on the switch underneath to alter the different lighting patterns. It’s gimmicky, but I’m sure they are targeting that photo crowd. The drinks themselves were OK. I think I prefer Brazilian style fruit cocktails now. The hamachi cevichee was decent. It’s just tartare with a different flavor profile, but otherwise, it tasted like what you’d expect.

item.title: Yoon Haeundae Galbi
item.description: Summary The food was actually surprisingly good. I would come back here again for Korean. The galbi was pretty tasty, and I think the price wasn’t too expensive I think. The meat quality is good. I’ve been back here twice already as I enjoyed this more than the other Ktown BBQ joints. Final Verdict I keep recommending this place over other KBBQ places not only because there is less wait but because the food quality is superb.

item.title: Bonchon
item.description: Pretty bad and expensive. 10 pieces of bites fro $14, and it doesn’t even come with a drink.

item.title: Samwon Garden Bbq
item.description: Pretty decent Korean BBQ. Also a bit expensive, and not as good as Let’s Meat. Let’s Meat

item.title: Let's Meat
item.description: Pretty decent Korean BBQ. A bit expensive, but everything was good.

item.title: Her Name Is Han
item.description: Pretty good Korean food. Fairly priced and family style. They have BOSSAM, and it was really good.

item.title: Barn Joo
item.description: Pretty good Korean American bar food outside of k-town. Not too expensive I think.

item.title: To Two Boonsik
item.description: This place is ok. Got the japchae and soondobu. Cheap though.

item.title: Tofu Tofu
item.description: Pretty good soon dobu and bo ssam. Lots of food for 1 person. Would come back here again if I was craving bo ssasm I think.

item.title: Jeju Noodle Bar
item.description: Pretty good Korean ramyun. A little pricey though.

item.title: Atoboy
item.description: Really good for the price point. $39 tasting 3 courses, no tip, and no dessert.

item.title: Bulbap Grill
item.description: This place was ok in Greenpoint. The soondobu was probably the better thing to get. They do have Korean style tacos.

item.title: Jongro
item.description: Pretty good. No Korean rice wrap, but meat quality was good.

item.title: Mokbar
item.description: It’s right across from the Los Tacos place in Chelsea Market. Got the Korean ramen, and it was abysmal.

item.title: Pelicana Chicken
item.description: Korean wings. They have ice cream beer which was a gimmick. It’s mediocre at best. They do give you these finger “condom” looking things to handle the chicken.

item.title: Kichin
item.description: Decent bibimbap, but don’t go out of your way for this.

item.title: Dotory
item.description: Really good. I like their kimchi jigae because it’s super sour. Don’t get their soondobu. It sucks.
